title: 帷幕
summary:
Captain Hastings mentally evaluates several potential suspects for the impending murder, considering Lieutenant Colonel Lutterell, Mr. Norton, Boyd Cardington, Dr. Franklin, and Colonel Alton. He also considers Miss Cole a possible female suspect, noting her tense and witch-like quality. While Hastings is lost in reminiscence regarding Cynthia Murdoch, Judith interrupts him to confront him about his strange behavior during lunch.

The conversation shifts to a past murder involving a kind, generous woman named Mrs. Inglescott, who was killed with cyanide. Hastings explains that Mrs. Inglescott's stepchildren felt like prisoners due to her control over the family wealth. Judith expresses intense indignation toward such "tyranny," arguing that the elderly should not enslave the young. She becomes agitated, comparing such people to "vampires," and praises Margaret Ritchfield, a woman who killed her cruel, controlling father and turned herself in. Hastings reprimands Judith for defending such dangerous notions.

Judith then informs Hastings that Mrs. Franklin wishes to see him in her bedroom. Upon arriving, Hastings finds Barbara Franklin resting on a sofa bed, accompanied by Mr. Franklin and Boyd Cardington. Mrs. Franklin introduces her nurse, Miss Crawford, a tall, beautiful young woman with reddish-brown hair and notably long, white hands. Miss Crawford remains silent during the introduction, merely tilting her head.
